items.title: Commit the lock
items.prefer: Commit poetry.lock so installs are reproducible.
items.why: Guarantees identical versions across machines and CI.
items.example: Verify: `git ls-files poetry.lock` should print the path. `.gitignore` should NOT mention `poetry.lock` for an application repo. (Libraries publishing to PyPI often don't commit it; apps always do.)

items.title: Let Poetry own deps
items.avoid: Mixing pip install into a Poetry project.
items.why: Keeps pyproject.toml and the lock authoritative.
items.example: Need redis? `poetry add redis` (updates pyproject + lock). Not `poetry run pip install redis` (drift between local env and lock — broken reproducibility).

items.title: Use groups
items.avoid: Lumping dev tools with runtime deps.
items.example:
```toml
[tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies]
pytest = "^8"
mypy = "^1.10"
```
Prod `poetry install --without dev` drops them; dev `poetry install` includes them.

items.title: Keep manifest and lock in sync
items.why: Avoids drift between intent and installed versions.
items.example: Bumped a version range manually in `pyproject.toml`? Always follow with `poetry lock --no-update` (re-resolve respecting new range) and commit both files together.
